title: The Electric Car
description: For questions 1–8, read the text below and choose which answer (A, B, C or D) best completes each gap.

content.text: Many people assume that electric vehicles are a recent invention, but they actually (0) date back to the nineteenth century. The first practical electric car was (1) developed in the 1880s, and by 1900, electric vehicles (2) accounted for a third of all cars on American roads. However, the discovery of cheap petroleum and improvements in internal combustion engines soon (3) put electric cars out of business. It was not (4) until the late twentieth century that interest in electric vehicles was revived. Growing (5) concerns about climate change and air pollution have driven demand for cleaner transportation. Modern electric cars can now (6) cover distances of over 500 kilometres on a single charge. (7) Furthermore, charging infrastructure is expanding rapidly in cities worldwide. While challenges remain, (8) particularly regarding battery production and recycling, the future of electric transportation looks increasingly promising.

example.explanation: 'Date back to' es la expresión correcta para indicar el origen temporal de algo.

questions.explanation: 'Developed' es el verbo más apropiado para el proceso de crear tecnología.
'Accounted for' significa 'representar' una proporción o porcentaje.
'Put out of business' es la expresión fija que significa hacer que algo deje de funcionar comercialmente.
'It was not until' es una estructura que enfatiza el momento en que algo finalmente ocurrió.
'Concerns about' es la colocación natural para preocupaciones sobre temas importantes.
'Cover distances' es la colocación correcta para recorrer distancias.
'Furthermore' añade información adicional que refuerza el punto anterior.
'Particularly' se usa para destacar un aspecto específico dentro de una categoría general.
